title: United to halt JFK service as airline battles FAA to add more flights
text: United Airlines said on Friday it will suspend service in late October to New York's John F. Kennedy Airport.

title: United is pulling out of New York's JFK airport indefinitely weeks after threatening to do so
text: United announced a plan to exit JFK International Airport in late October on Friday but vowed that the airline will return.

title: United Airlines will halt service at New York's JFK airport
text: 100 United Airlines employees affected by FAA's current inability to give United more slots at JFK. The air carrier remained optimistic about more future slots.

title: United Airlines Holdings Stock Looks Set For A Come Back
text: United Airlines Holdings Inc. stock (NASDAQ: UAL) is down 6.1% in the past week, strongly underperforming the S&P 500 which was down 1.8% over this period.

title: United Airlines (UAL) to Resume US Flights to Cuba by Year End
text: United Airlines (UAL) flight resumption to Cuba should create aviation jobs and multiple other indirect vacancies.

text: Macroeconomic conditions are making it hard for this sector to take flight.

title: 3 Airline Stocks to Watch Amid Air-Travel Demand Strength
text: Key players in the Zacks Transportation -- Airline industry like Delta (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and JetBlue (JBLU) are likely to benefit immensely from increased passenger volumes.

title: Why United Airlines Stock Was Slipping Today
text: 25 planes were grounded on Monday.

title: United Airlines (UAL) Calls Off Flights on Inspection Failure
text: Cancellation of flights on grounds of inspection failure looks disappointing for United Airlines (UAL).

title: What The U.S. Airline Industry Could Look Like In 2035
text: The airline industry in always changing. By 2035, there will likely be fewer airlines, more airport automation, more efficient fleets, and lower fares for consumers.

title: United Airlines grounds planes as it completes inspections
text: United Airlines pulled some jets from service recently for required inspections, affecting 25 flights. The rate of flight delays is up 23% for all airlines since before the pandemic.

title: United Airlines grounds 25 planes after missed inspections
text: CHICAGO — United Airlines canceled about 18 flights this week when it realized that some of its Boeing 777-200 planes had not undergone required inspections of the front edges of the wings.

title: United CEO on FAA air traffic controllers: 'They need more'
text: United Airlines considers government air traffic controller staffing shortages its top concern and says there is still time to minimize the impact on holiday travel this winter.

title: United Airlines And Emirates Kiss And Make Up. ‘If You Can't Beat Them, Join Them.
text: Forgive United Airlines' unions for being puzzled that United and Emirates are now friends, after years of being enemies.

title: United just announced a landmark nonstop route to Dubai in new partnership with Emirates
text: The new daily flight will connect United customers to over 100 cities in Asia and beyond as part of a new codeshare agreement with Emirates.

title: United Airlines partners with one-time foe Emirates, will launch Dubai flights
text: United Airlines and Emirates announced a codeshare partnership, which allows the carriers to sell seats on each other's flights.

text: Economic uncertainty is making it hard for this sector to take off.

title: United Airlines hoping you'll pay $100 to ride this to the airport
text: The taxis will take off and land vertically, like helicopters.

title: United Airlines (UAL) Stock Up on Upbeat 3Q Revenue View
text: United Airlines (UAL) anticipates third-quarter 2022 total operating revenues to grow almost 12% from the September quarter of 2019.

title: United Airlines invests $15 million in Eve Air, orders 200 air taxis
text: U.S. carrier United Airlines Holdings Inc said on Thursday it would invest $15 million in air-taxi maker Eve Air Mobility and ordered 200 aircraft from the Embraer-owned startup.

title: United Airlines makes another investment in flying taxis, with $15 million going to Eve Air Mobility
text: United Airlines Holdings Inc. UAL, +5.52% said Thursday it made a $15 million investment in flying taxi maker Eve Air Mobility. The air carrier has entered into a conditional purchase agreement for 200 four-seat electric aircraft and 200 options, with the first deliveries expected as early as 2026.

title: United Airlines raises bet on electric air taxis with 200 aircraft from upstart Eve
text: United will invest $15 million in startup Eve and order 200 of the four-seat aircraft.

title: Why United Airlines Stock Gained Altitude Today
text: Demand is holding up well even as the summer travel season fades.

title: U.S. must do more to block harmful airline mergers, anti-monopoly group says
text: The U.S. Transportation Department (USDOT) must step up efforts to stop anti-competitive U.S. airline mergers and hold carriers accountable for flight cancellations and poor customer service, an influential Washington-based think-thank said on Wednesday.

title: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (UAL) Presents at Cowen 14th Annual Global Transportation & Sustainable Mobility Conference (Transcript)
text: United Airlines Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:UAL ) Cowen 14th Annual Global Transportation & Sustainable Mobility Conference September 7, 2022 11:20 AM ET Company Participants Gerald Laderman - Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer Patrick Quill - Senior Vice President of Global Network Planning and Alliances Conference Call Participants Helane Becker - Cowen Helane Becker Good morning, everybody. title: Here's why airline stocks are taking off this week
text: Airline stocks outperformed the broader equity market on Wednesday, getting a boost from a rosier outlook for United Airlines Holdings Inc. and news that U.S. airports recently welcomed more travelers than they had before the pandemic.

title: Holiday air travel tops pre-pandemic levels for the 1st time
text: For the first time since COVID-19 brought air travel to a standstill, the number of people streaming through U.S. airport security checkpoints over a holiday weekend exceeded pre-pandemic levels.

title: United Airlines Still Sees 'Strong Demand,' Raises Q3 Revenue Forecast
text: United Airlines slightly raised Q3 revenue targets, saying it still sees "strong demand." UAL stock was little changed early Wednesday.

title: United Airlines Raises Revenue Outlook Due to 'Strong' Demand
text: The airline also has reportedly threatened to suspend its service at New York's John F. Kennedy airport next month unless the Federal Aviation Authority grants the carrier more flights.

title: United Airlines stock rallies as 'strong' demand leads to raised revenue growth outlook
text: Shares of United Airlines Holdings Inc. UAL, +3.60% rose 1.4% in premarket trading Wednesday, after the air carrier raised its third-quarter revenue growth outlook, citing continued "strong" demand exiting a "robust" summer. The company now expects revenue to be about 12% above the same period in pre-pandemic 2019, compared with previous growth guidance of about 11%.

title: United Airlines Threatens to Stop JFK Service Unless FAA Approves More Flights
text: United Airlines has reportedly threatened to suspend its service at New York's John F. Kennedy airport next month unless the Federal Aviation Authority grants the carrier more flights.

title: United Airlines may end JFK service without more flights
text: United Airlines has warned the FAA if it doesn't get approved for more flights, it will discontinue service to New York's JFK Airport in October.

title: United Airlines threatens to drop JFK service if U.S. does not approve more flights
text: United Airlines said on Tuesday it plans to end services in October to New York's John F. Kennedy Airport (JFK) if the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) does not grant the air carrier additional flights.

title: Labor Day weekend air travel surpasses 2019 levels as airlines cap a rocky summer
text: It was the first holiday weekend since the pandemic began that TSA screenings topped 2019 tallies, a milestone in air travel's bumpy recovery.

title: Airlines count on business travelers to keep recovery going
text: With summer vacations winding down, airlines are counting on the return of more business travelers to keep their pandemic recovery going into the fall.

title: What Awaits U.S. Airlines This Labor Day Travel Period?
text: United Airlines (UAL), American Airlines (AAL) and Delta (DAL) expect to carry 2.6 million, 2.5 million and 2.9 million passengers, respectively, during the Labor Day holiday period.
